mappers.what_it_is: The repo's automation control plane: five GitHub Actions workflows (all cron-scheduled or manual dispatch), one local pre-commit language gate, a Copilot instruction file, and a governance PR template. Workflows run governance/verifier scripts on a timer and upload receipts as run artifacts; the pre-commit hook runs the language gate on staged prose/receipt files. Notably, NOTHING is enforced automatically on push or pull_request via Actions — the PR-time gating surface is entirely empty.

current_state.note: 5 live workflows but zero run on push/PR (all cron + workflow_dispatch). brain-loop-autorun (every 30m) is LIVE and can deploy to Cloudflare when CF_* secrets are set (BRAIN_CI_AUTONOMOUS=1) — do not touch. p0pgr-shadow-cycle is scaffolded+locked: dispatch-only, cron commented pending FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3-CRON-SHADOW receipt, shadow=compile/lint/route with zero execution. census/staleness/auth-probe are live scheduled. The pre-commit hook exists but is INERT here: core.hooksPath is unset and no installer wires it. PR template + copilot forbidden-marker rules are documented but unenforced by any CI.

key_assets.purpose: Every-30-min autorun; checks out SourceA, installs wrangler, runs scripts/brain_loop_autorun_v1.sh; deploys to Cloudflare when CF_MAIN_TOKEN+CF_VERIFIER_TOKEN present (BRAIN_CI_AUTONOMOUS=1). LIVE-DEPLOY BOUNDARY — do not modify.

Every-6h auth surface probe via scripts/verify_auth_surfaces_e2e_v1.py; exits 1 on FAIL, optional --fail-on-warn strict gate. The only workflow that actually fails/blocks on a status.
Weekly (Mon 08:00) WORKFLOW_CENSUS_v1: idempotent Supabase migration + census write (--write-supabase), continue-on-error, uploads census receipt.
Weekly (Mon 09:00) agent-read staleness inspect via run_agent_read_governance_cycle_v1.sh; uploads staleness receipt + governance_stale_pointer_queue_v1.json.

Local pre-commit language gate: runs language_gate/language_gate_pipeline_v1.py on staged .md/.mdc/.txt/.json (skips language_gate/receipts/ + node_modules). Present but not wired (core.hooksPath unset).
Governance PR template: required lane declaration, motor_id_or_human_gate, task cell, receipt_id proof, and two non-duplication checkboxes. Machine-parseable fields, but no CI parses/enforces them.
Agent/Copilot rules: repo-local changes, receipts immutable, canonical repo identity, and forbidden active-config marker 'kazemnezhadsina144-dot'. Unenforced by CI.

mappers.what_it_is: A deterministic, LLM-free 8-stage Python pipeline that turns messy decision backlogs (form rows, chat forks, audit findings, agent questions, markdown bullets) into a reduced founder-review sheet plus a validation-gated apply-map. Each stage emits a JSON receipt. Its stated thesis is "the form is not the product, the machine is the product" — it normalizes raw statements, rewrites jargon to plain English, checks terminology against the sibling language_gate dictionary, clusters duplicates, classifies each item into MACHINE_VALIDATABLE / ADVISOR_REVIEW / FOUNDER_FACT / DEFER, and only presents advisor clusters + founder facts to the founder while machine-closing the rest.

current_state.note: Runs end-to-end via `python3 dlm_v1.py <input>`; 8 committed sample runs exist under output/ and receipts/ (committed Jul 8 2026, commit 4ad2f5c). It is a working v1 but rule-based: clustering (THEME_RULES) and classification (FOUNDER_FACT/DEFER/ADVISOR/MACHINE_EVIDENCE pattern lists) are hardcoded regex tuned to one founder corpus, so it won't generalize to new corpora without editing those lists. Two hard couplings/caveats: (1) dlm_terms_v1.py imports the OUT-OF-SCOPE sibling language_gate/ (language_gate_core_v1.py + dictionary_index.json) for the dictionary check — that dependency exists on disk and resolves; (2) committed output manifests carry a stale absolute path (/Users/.../Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/...) from an earlier machine, so old artifacts are cosmetically stale though the code recomputes paths correctly at runtime. The apply-map stage is deliberately fenced: it never auto-submits FORM_OFFICIAL and blocks on unvalidated advisor/founder picks unless partial_batch is on.

mappers.subsystem: Edge verifier worker + Supabase census layer (workers/ + infrastructure/)
mappers.what_it_is: Two coupled pieces of the governance SSOT's trust/judging layer. (1) workers/github-app-advisory is a Cloudflare Worker acting as the INDEPENDENT second-account verifier in the brain-worker promotion chain: it authenticates as GitHub App 4179901 (installation 143449507), mints an installation token at the edge, pulls the SSOT and candidate artifacts (knowledge bundles + brain-worker code) from Noetfield-Systems/sina-governance-SSOT and Noetfield-Systems/SourceA, recomputes SHA256 over them, validates them against a declared artifact descriptor, and writes a PASS/FAIL/MATCH/BLOCKED advisory receipt to KV. PASS is only granted when it proves it ran on the SECONDARY CF account (b7282b4a5c17b84d62e3ef8866b878f8) and at the edge (cf-ray + WORKER_URL match) — i.e. runtime separation from the main SourceA account. (2) infrastructure/supabase/migrations/001_workflow_census_v1.sql is the WORKFLOW_CENSUS_v1 judging schema (per-loop rows + weekly rollups with deterministic GREEN/YELLOW/RED audit flags) that names every loop's receipt and value_class, populated weekly into the noetfield Supabase project.

mappers.subsystem: In-repo Agent Skills + macOS desktop eval/audit apps (the auditing & skill-packaging layer)
mappers.what_it_is: Seven Claude Code Agent Skills (SKILL.md = YAML frontmatter + markdown playbook) that wrap the repo's Python governance-enforcement scripts so an agent runs and interprets them correctly (real numbers + escalation posture) instead of skimming for a bare PASS/FAIL. Four of the audit skills are also packaged as double-click macOS .app bundles: a bash launcher runs a generate.py that shells the underlying script read-only and renders a live HTML dashboard to /tmp. One skill (pr-conflict-resolver) additionally ships an eval harness proving a +0.24 pass-rate lift versus no-skill.

current_state.note: Skills themselves are LIVE and complete: every script/data file they wrap was verified to exist on disk (audit_receipt_ledger_v1.py, agent_read_staleness_engine_v1.py, verify_agent_read_staleness_v1.sh, validate_parallel_automation_governance_v1.py, audit_automation_surface_v1.py, check.py, and all 5 referenced data/*.json). BUT: (1) only 1 of the 4 audit skills (pr-conflict-resolver) has an evals/ harness — the other three have none. (2) All 4 desktop apps HARDCODE REPO=/Users/sinakazemnezhad/Desktop/Noetfield-Systems/sina-governance-SSOT (the founder's Desktop clone, NOT this worktree) — verified that path exists, so they run only on the founder's Mac against a different checkout. (3) The PR-Conflict-Resolver app is the odd one out: it ships a frozen 85KB static report.html and does NOT regenerate, while the other three regenerate live each launch. (4) .app bundles are unsigned and hand-maintained — no build/packaging script exists in-tree; STYLE CSS + launcher bash are copy-pasted across bundles. Note: the four audit skills are in-repo files but are NOT in this session's active skill list, whereas the adjacent p0pgr-* trio is.

mappers.what_it_is: The trust boundary between a sandbox-authored candidate artifact (knowledge_bundle / brain_worker_bundle) and a live production Worker deploy. An independent verifier — a Cloudflare Worker running on a SECONDARY CF account (b7282b4a...), authing via a GitHub App installation token, whose source lives remotely in SourceA (not in this repo) — emits signed receipts proving edge execution, account independence, and candidate-hash match. The promotion gate (gates/promotion_gate.py) is a local Python CLI that fetches that receipt and REFUSES to deploy unless it passes a battery of invariants (status/result==PASS, pass_claimed, edge_execution_proven, secondary_account_proven, cf_account_id, candidate_ref/path/sha256, no failures), then optionally executes a founder-confirmed / semi-auto-window / autonomous deploy and writes a deploy receipt. verifier/ holds the specs, JSON Schema, and independence birth-receipt; gates/ holds the enforcement engine.

current_state.note: The gate (gates/promotion_gate.py, 763 lines) is LIVE and exercised: it has real callers (scripts/promote_brain_worker_v1.sh) and has produced real deploy receipts (receipts/phase0.3-step10a-watched-deploy-receipt.json shows deploy_executed:true, and even caught a FAILED_INVARIANT content-identity mismatch). Confirm-each-time and semi-auto-window modes work. But the AUTONOMOUS deploy path is LOCKED behind flags (~/.sina/brain-autonomous-deploy-v1.flag) and step7 note states autonomous gate-triggered deploy is Step 10, 'founder-gated and not yet authorized'. The verifier side (verifier/) is SPEC/SCAFFOLD: knowledge-bundle-spec-v0.1.md and brain-worker-code-spec-v0.1.md are v0.1 SPEC docs ('Status: SUBMITTED / SPEC / NO LIVE DEPLOY'); the code that actually ENFORCES those specs is not in verifier/ (it lives in the remote Worker). The birth receipt is REMOTE_CHECK_ADVISORY with pass_claimed:false. Live Cloudflare deploys and the P0-PGR runtime are locked/out-of-scope.

key_assets.purpose: The entire promotion gate: refusal_reasons() (receipt PASS/FAIL invariants), independence/rollback/autonomous refusals, source_refusal_reasons() (clean-main + dirty-tree guard + committed-vs-worktree-vs-expected sha256), bundle-artifacts-only allowlist, execute_deploy_flow() (pre/post live version diff, IDENTITY_MISMATCH, health, brain-live smoke, post-promote hook, mac SIGKILL recovery, autonomous-hold-on-fail), write_deploy_receipt(). Emits PROMOTION_GATE: REFUSED / BLOCKED / APPROVED_* lines with exit codes 2-6.

Loads CF_MAIN_TOKEN / CF_VERIFIER_TOKEN / CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN from ~/.sina/secrets/cloudflare-tokens.env into env without printing values; the main-vs-verifier token split enforces the two-account independence boundary.

SPEC for a knowledge_bundle candidate: two accepted shapes (original proposal + current SourceA Brain), size bounds (2 bytes-1MB, <=2000 chunks, <=20000 char/chunk), must-parse rules, required chunk metadata + content_sha256, hash rule (submitted bytes SHA256 must equal descriptor proposed_sha256), and the 11 required verifier-receipt fields. Prose only; no executable validator in-repo.

SPEC for brain_worker_bundle (4-file deploy set: index.js, guardrails.js, brain-core-gate-v1.js, knowledge-bundle.json). Defines the three-hash rule: worker_code_sha256 and proposed_sha256 = SHA256 of a compact, sorted-keys JSON object mapping each path->its SHA256. PASS when remote bytes at candidate_ref match all three hashes + bundle validation. Points to receipts/verifier-independence-proof-latest.json.

JSON Schema 2020-12 for the sandbox->verifier submission descriptor. additionalProperties:false; author_id const 'sandbox', subject const 'live Worker', artifact_type const 'knowledge_bundle', candidate_repo enum (SSOT or SourceA), proposed/base sha256 patterns. This is the machine-checkable candidate contract.

The independence birth proof: REMOTE_CHECK_ADVISORY, verifier_runtime cloudflare_worker, credential github_app_installation_token, secondary cf_account b7282b4a..., edge proof via cf_ray/cf_colo=YVR/TLSv1.3, edge_execution_proven+secondary_account_proven=true, pass_eligible:true but pass_claimed:false (advisory-only).

Records the live Brain baseline (514 chunks live, version 628ebc37..., MAIN CF account 0d0b967b vs verifier secondary b7282b4a — 'must not be merged'), that Step 7 was a founder-approved MANUAL deploy (not gate-triggered), and names the next step: 'Step 8: gate refusal teeth — feed a non-PASS receipt and prove the gate blocks deploy; does not require the live SourceA repo.'

SEAM (out of gates/ but consumed by it): the gate's default --independence-receipt-path. Must be result/status==PASS, secondary_account_proven=true, recorded_at fresh (<=30d default) or the gate refuses any deploy. Regenerated by scripts/prove_verifier_independence_v1.sh.

SEAM: the sandbox registry. Each row (brain_worker, knowledge_bundle, locked_definitions, contract_pages, pattern_export) carries a gate_profile (deploy_command, source_bundle_path, bundle_artifacts_only, post_promote_command, brain_live_smoke_default) that apply_sandbox_profile() loads by --sandbox-id. Adding a row creates a new promotion lane with zero gate code change.

SEAM: the flag API the gate imports — autonomous_deploy_enabled(), autonomous_hold_active(), set_autonomous_hold(), clear_autonomous_hold(), mutation_trials_enabled(). Backed by ~/.sina/brain-autonomous-deploy-v1.flag and ~/.sina/enforcement/brain-autonomous-hold-v1.flag; this is the lock keeping autonomous deploy disabled.

extension_points: Sandbox registry rows in data/brain_domain_sandboxes_v1.json -> gate_profile, loaded by apply_sandbox_profile(): a new row adds a full promotion lane (deploy_command, source_bundle_path, post_promote_command, bundle_artifacts_only, smoke default) without touching gate code.

artifact_type dispatch inside refusal_reasons(): currently handles 'knowledge_bundle' and adds extra hash checks (worker_code_sha256, knowledge_bundle_sha256) for 'brain_worker_bundle'. New artifact types plug in here paired with a new spec doc in verifier/.

The verifier->gate receipt field contract (status, result, pass_claimed, edge_execution_proven, secondary_account_proven, cf_account_id, candidate_ref/path/sha256, candidate_validation_failures, failures): any new deploy invariant = one new emitted field + one new check line.
The descriptor JSON Schema (verifier/brain-config-artifact-descriptor-schema-v0.1.json): the machine-checkable sandbox-submission contract; new fields/artifact_type consts extend it.

post_promote_command hook in execute_deploy_flow()/run_post_promote_command(): runs arbitrary post-deploy validation (e.g. promote_locked_definitions_v1.py, validate-sourcea-contract-pages-e2e-v1.sh) — a generic post-promotion validator seam per sandbox.
Independence proof gate independence_refusal_reasons(): consumes receipts/verifier-independence-proof-latest.json with a configurable --independence-max-age-days freshness window.

Deploy-receipt output schema in write_deploy_receipt(): structured JSON receipts (receipt_type, deploy_executed, content_identity_ok, versions, smoke, post_promote) written to disk for downstream auditors.

buildable_ideas.idea: Build the missing executable knowledge-bundle validator: a pure-offline Python module that turns knowledge-bundle-spec-v0.1.md + brain-worker-code-spec-v0.1.md into enforced checks (UTF-8 decode, JSON-object root, size bounds <=1MB / <=2000 chunks / <=20000 char per chunk, BOTH accepted chunk shapes, content_sha256/content_hash format, submitted-bytes SHA256 == descriptor proposed_sha256). Today the spec is prose and the enforcing validator only exists in the remote Worker. Emits the 11 spec-required receipt fields as a local advisory receipt.

Implement the step7 note's explicit next task — 'Step 8: gate refusal teeth' — as a pytest harness that feeds crafted non-PASS receipts (status!=PASS, missing edge_execution_proven, wrong cf_account_id, mismatched candidate_sha256, non-empty failures, stale/absent independence proof, autonomous-hold flag active) into refusal_reasons()/main() with --receipt-url pointed at a local mock, asserting REFUSED and exit code 2 for each. step7 note states this 'does not require the live SourceA repo'.

Ship a three-hash descriptor computer CLI implementing the brain-worker-code-spec exactly: build the sorted-keys, compact-separator JSON path->SHA256 map and hash it to produce worker_code_sha256 and proposed_sha256, so a sandbox author can generate a spec-correct descriptor and self-verify hashes offline BEFORE submitting to the verifier. The spec fully defines the algorithm but no committed tool computes it.

Sandbox-registry gate-profile linter: statically validate every row of brain_domain_sandboxes_v1.json against what apply_sandbox_profile() consumes — deploy_root expands, deploy_command is never the banned 'bash scripts/brain_cli_v1.sh deploy' (which execute_deploy_flow refuses), source_bundle_path present when bundle_artifacts_only, post_promote_command script path exists, health_url well-formed. Catches a misconfigured lane before any real gate run.

Wire brain-config-artifact-descriptor-schema-v0.1.json into a jsonschema-based descriptor validator plus a valid/invalid fixture generator, so submissions are checked against additionalProperties:false, author_id const 'sandbox', subject const 'live Worker', candidate_repo enum, and sha256 patterns before the gate ever fetches a receipt. Makes adding a new artifact_type a one-const change.

Deploy-receipt auditor: a read-only rollup over receipts/*deploy-receipt*.json that reproduces the gate's own success logic (content_identity_ok, deploy_exit_code, smoke ok, post_promote ok) and classifies each as PASS/FAIL/BLOCKED with the exact rollback_hint. Grounded in a real produced failure — phase0.3-step10a-watched-deploy-receipt.json has content_identity_confirmed:false + gate_failure_after_deploy — so it immediately surfaces already-recorded FAILED_INVARIANT deploys.

mappers.what_it_is: The 50-script motor layer that drives the governance SSOT: it runs the Mac/CI brain autorun loop (verify → self-heal → matrix → gated promote), the P0-PGR shadow dispatch pipeline (evidence → lint → route → receipt), the weekly workflow census + agent-read staleness governance cycles, auth/Supabase e2e probes, receipt-ledger audits, and heuristic chess-risk pre-checks. Every motor is receipt-emitting and registry-driven; most transitions are deterministic shell/Python with no LLM calls, so shadow cost is a true 0.0.

current_state.note: Mixed maturity. LIVE + scheduled: brain_loop_autorun (CI cron */30 + Mac launchd), workflow_census (weekly), agent_read_staleness (weekly Mon), sg_auth_surface_probe (every 6h). SCAFFOLDED/shadow-only: entire p0pgr_* pipeline — shadow-cycle CI is workflow_dispatch ONLY, the cron is commented pending a FOUNDER-UNLOCK-R3 receipt, and the cycle does route-decision-only with zero execution (matches memory: P0-PGR runtime scaffolded, execution locked). LOCKED for sandbox purposes: promote_brain_worker --autonomous-deploy (touches live Cloudflare sourcea-brain-chat-v1 Worker via promotion_gate) and the autonomous-hold path in brain_loop_autorun. STALE/stub: write_roi_heartbeat emits all-"unknown" metrics; living_system_chain_validate is an LS-021 stub. audit_* tools always exit 0 (surface findings, never gate).

key_assets.purpose: Core 30m autorun motor. Sequences _run_step self-heal → parallel candidates → e2e matrix, then either OBSERVE_ONLY or (founder-flag) autonomous promote. Reads flags under ~/.sina/, writes receipts/brain-loop-autorun-*.json. This is the heartbeat of the whole layer.

Shared sourced lib for all brain_* / promote / validate shell motors: SourceA worktree resolution, TCC/SIGKILL (rc 137/139) handling, overlap-lock (brain_acquire_lock), BRAIN_PYTHON pinning. Single point every shell motor depends on.

Deploy gate wrapper — resolves candidate ref/sha from live verifier receipt, invokes gates/promotion_gate.py with confirm-each-time or --autonomous-deploy to the secondary CF account. The one script that reaches live Cloudflare; LOCKED for sandbox work.

Self-heal tick: compares live verifier receipt candidate_sha256/ref vs local bundle_sha256 + git HEAD (ancestor logic) to decide skip vs reverify, optionally re-triggers /run. Writes brain-self-heal-tick receipt.

SSOT loader for data/brain_domain_sandboxes_v1.json — get_sandbox, resolve_sourcea_root, git_ref, bundle_sha256. Imported by self-heal, trigger_verifier_run, parallel candidates. Registry is the plug-point for new brain sandboxes.

P0-PGR shadow cycle motor: evidence → load packet → lint → ROUTE-DECISION-ONLY → schema-validated loop-state receipt (receipts/p0pgr/). Non-binary verdicts (PASS/REPAIR_CANDIDATE/DEGRADED); bad packet becomes a repair_candidates file, lane never stops. Zero execution in Phase-0.

Packet linter: jsonschema + 7 semantic rules (delivery fields, HARD_BLOCK enum, CLOUD_ONLY/cloud_safe, HYBRID_MAC reqs, FOUNDER_ONLY for deploy/authority, P0-CORE leak fingerprints). HARD_BLOCK_REASONS/DELIVERY_REQUIRED are the rule tables.

Deterministic ROI ranker (move M10): WEIGHTS + authored FACTORS table (0-1) over campaign moves → ranked cloud-only queue + HOLD_CLOUD_UNSAFE for Mac-bound. Emits receipts/p0pgr/phase2_queue_v1.json. Recommendation only — does not flip dispatch flags.

Crawls all motors (GH Actions, CF workers, launchd), classifies each by which receipt it serves + value_class, writes Supabase + census receipt. Reads data/automation_surface_inventory + workflow_census_extensions + value_class_rules. Weekly CI.

Scans receipts/ for collision/anomaly classes (malformed JSON, missing timestamp, near-simultaneous different-identity receipts within 5s, duplicate basename across dirs). Always exit 0 — audit, not gate. TIMESTAMP_KEYS + checks are extensible.

Heuristic risk pre-check: reads mission/raw_move JSON, matches RISK_WORDS (clean/simplify/…) + IRREVERSIBLE_HINTS (delete/send/spend/…), emits a CHESS_PASS skeleton with likely_misread/second-move-risk and PROCEED/PROCEED_WITH_PATCH/ASK_IF_IRREVERSIBLE action. Standalone stdin/file CLI.

Alive-doc law motor: reads data/agent_read_surfaces_v1.json + stale_law_guard_patterns, flags stale governance pointers, files into governance_stale_pointer_queue, emits receipt. Weekly cycle via run_agent_read_governance_cycle_v1.sh.

mappers.what_it_is: The ratified single-source-of-truth store for the governance system's laws, schemas, templates and data-registries across authority tiers P0-P8. It holds the constitution/invariants (P0), operational canon + machine-loop registry (P1), ratified SSOT rules (P2), cloud-kernel PDFs (P4), the Line-Engine execution architecture + a working Prompt-Forge compiler (P5), locked brain definitions (P6), the thinking-law doctrine + language layer (P7), and the validation/critique/repair loop specs (P8). mappers.what_it_is: A fail-closed wording-authority gate. It builds a canonical plain-English dictionary index from an A-Z doctrine batch, then regex-lints any file for retired aliases, banned register, overclaims, private-only leakage, and undefined Title-Case/ALLCAPS system terms; a deterministic agent pass rewrites to public phrasing and emits a review sidecar. Every scan writes a JSON receipt and returns exit 1 on FAIL (and on WARN when strict), which is what git/Cursor hooks enforce.

Build scripts/p0pgr_repair_relint_v1.py: read each file in receipts/p0pgr/repair_candidates/, re-run lint_packet on the referenced packet, and emit a resolved/persisting/new report receipt. Closes the 'rejection re-enters the loop as repair' loop that the cycle currently only half-implements (it files candidates but never re-processes them).

Build a batch outbox linter: the R2 workflow cycles only ONE packet at a time, but 10 packets sit in the outbox and the mac_runner fallback_route was bulk-removed (phase2_scorecard notes a re-lint). A script that lints every receipts/p0pgr/outbox/*.json and emits a batch report catches drift across the whole queue in one pass.

Build a deterministic weekly founder ROI digest generator (markdown/HTML) that the R5 rung explicitly calls for: summarize ranked moves, executed candidates (M10 ranker, M03 PARTIAL), findings needing founder eyes (F1 pricing ambiguity, F2 doc-stale v71->v108), held cloud-unsafe items, and running cost/send/deploy counters — all already present in the queue + scorecard + exec receipt.

categories.name: New Gates & Validators (turn prose specs into executable checks)
categories.why_this_category: The single most recurring gap across every subsystem: a spec/schema exists and is treated as authority, but the enforcing code lives remotely, in a comment, or nowhere. These fill specified-but-unbuilt enforcement seams, all read-only/advisory, all Phase-0 safe.

ideas.title: Build the missing P0-PGR Receipt Verifier (pipeline stage 11)
ideas.description: scripts/p0pgr_receipt_verify_v1.py: a read-only adversarial checker validating any execution receipt against P0_EXECUTION_RECEIPT_SCHEMA_v1.json plus provenance rules (executed_at not predating packet created_at, founder_authorization_ref present for real exec, evidence_artifacts present, cost never bare $0 without accounting_note). Emits a verdict receipt. The pipeline names stage 11 and the schema exists, but no script does it.
ideas.sandbox_first_step: Read P0_EXECUTION_RECEIPT_SCHEMA_v1.json and one real exec receipt (receipts/p0pgr/... M03), then scaffold a jsonschema+provenance checker that emits a verdict receipt to a scratch path.

Today this enforcement only exists in the remote CF Worker.
ideas.sandbox_first_step: Read both v0.1 spec docs, then write a hash-map computer that reproduces the sorted-keys/compact-separator path->SHA256 rule and self-checks against a local 4-file fixture bundle.

catalog.guardrails: LOCKED / do-not-touch in the sandbox: (1) P0-PGR real execution beyond cloud-only read-only ranking — deploy, merge, cron, auto-dispatch, and rungs R3/R4/R5 are all gated behind founder-unlock receipts that do not exist; never uncomment the shadow-cycle cron line, never flip dispatch_now, never set status AUTO_DISPATCH_APPROVED on a registry template. (2) Live Cloudflare deploys — promote_brain_worker --autonomous-deploy, gates/promotion_gate.py execute_deploy_flow against the secondary CF account, and the workers/github-app-advisory live worker (edit/redeploy) are founder-gated; build offline harnesses over the same code/schema instead. (3) brain-loop-autorun-v1.yml (every 30m, can deploy when CF_* secrets present) is a live-deploy boundary — do not modify. (4) No auto-merge / no self-merge of any PR. (5) Never delete artifacts — rejection is not permission to rm; receipts are append-only/immutable. (6) No writes to live Supabase (noetfield tkgpapowwplupyekpivy / workflow_census tables) — run census/probe scripts in --json report-only mode without --write-supabase/--write-receipt; use a sandbox DB for any census-row work. (7) No outbound sends — commercial_pulse send stays founder_blocked; dispatch-checks are dry-run/scoring only. (8) Do NOT wire new .cursor/hooks or pre-commit during dictionary/gate experiments — ship standalone CLIs. (9) Deploy fence blocks dirty-tree pushes; Cloudflare is retired for www (Cloudflare www + Railway backend is the real stack, never Vercel). (10) Deliverables are simple self-contained HTML pages, never .pptx decks; build the file, never push it live.

critique.verdict: The catalog is strong on per-subsystem enforcement/harness gaps but systematically under-weights cross-subsystem wiring (it treats all 10 subsystems as silos), governance-fence hardening inside DLM, protection of live commercial surfaces, and the deterministic differential tests that would prove its own offline reimplementations faithful — the highest-leverage plays sit precisely in those seams.
critique.strongest_pick: DLM → P0-PGR packet bridge: pipe DLM's MACHINE_VALIDATABLE / ADVISOR_REVIEW cluster output straight into p0pgr_campaign_planner_v1.py's compile_packet() as DRAFT, lint-only packets. It is the single biggest surface the whole catalog missed — DLM's classified, plain-English, de-duplicated decision items ARE the "problem statement" the P0-PGR packet compiler already consumes, so a thin adapter fuses the system's two flagship brains into one messy-backlog→dispatchable-work pipeline. Fully phase-safe (DRAFT status, linter-only, dispatch_now stays false), grounded in two live scripts, and turns two isolated demos into an actual assembly line.

This connects the two largest brains into one backlog→dispatch pipeline; phase-safe because packets ship DRAFT/lint-only and never AUTO_DISPATCH.
